Open mode bypasses the sandbox firewall entirely. The InstanceView shows a red banner each loop and an SP-I warning is appended to every review file while this is active — use it for explicit exploration, not as a default.

Orbis Dei is an autopoietic organism that writes, runs, and reviews itself one six-phase loop at a time. This app hosts many such organisms side by side — each isolated, with its own corpus, git history, and constitution.
Provider + model (from settings) per loop phase. The required API key must be set in settings.
Provider per loop phase. The model per provider comes from settings.

Runs model-generated shell scripts in a macOS sandbox-exec jail — network denied (unless changed below), writes confined to the instance directory, 30s timeout. Residual risk remains; a determined exploit could still find gaps. When off, tools are still written to disk but not executed.
SC-004: {n} consecutive unanimous elections — suspected filter failure. RSI diagnosis by the superinstance is indicated.
SC-004: {n} consecutive unanimous elections. At 20, automatic RSI diagnosis is expected.
SC-005: {n} loops without a new tool (limit 200). Diverge should synthesize a tool or document the abstinence.
SC-005: {n} loops without a new tool — approaching the 200 limit.
Sandbox allows TCP 80/443 only to the allowlisted hosts.
Sandbox denies all networking. Tools are fully isolated.
Sandbox firewall off. Tools have raw network access.
